output.summary: Fixed critical design issue: re-added desktop logo max-height constraint (md:**:max-h-[58px]) to prevent logo from stretching too tall. The logo now displays at the correct ~58px height on desktop while maintaining the two-row span via self-stretch.

criticalIssueFixed.problem: Logo was massively oversized on desktop (~500px tall, 8-9x the intended height) because the max-height constraint was removed in the previous edit. The logo stretched to fill the entire two-row height of the header.
criticalIssueFixed.root_cause: In the initial implementation of the 9 changes, I misunderstood the requirement 'logo spans into the two rows'. I removed the max-h-[58px] constraint thinking the logo should grow to fill the full row height. However, Figma shows the logo at ~58px tall which IS the correct two-row height. The logo should VISUALLY span the rows via the self-stretch property on the link container, but the IMAGE ITSELF should be constrained to 58px.
criticalIssueFixed.figma_value: Logo ~215px wide x ~58px tall, compact in left column
criticalIssueFixed.implementation_before: Logo stretched to ~500px tall, no max-height constraint on desktop
criticalIssueFixed.implementation_after: Logo constrained to 58px tall via md:**:max-h-[58px], maintains self-stretch container
criticalIssueFixed.fix: Re-added md:**:max-h-[58px] to logo classes in both PHP and TSX files

change.to: class="block self-stretch w-auto **:w-auto **:max-w-full **:h-full **:object-contain shrink-0 max-md:**:max-h-[26px] md:**:max-h-[58px]"

filesModified.notes: Added md:**:max-h-[58px] constraint to logo link element

change.to: className="block self-stretch w-auto **:w-auto **:max-w-full **:h-full **:object-contain shrink-0 max-md:**:max-h-[26px] md:**:max-h-[58px]"

filesModified.notes: Added md:**:max-h-[58px] constraint to both ImageDropUploader (line 592) and logo link (line 603)

desktop.rationale: Matches Figma design where logo is ~58px tall, which equals the combined height of the utility row + nav row
desktop.classes: self-stretch on container allows it to span the two-row height, but **:max-h-[58px] constrains the actual image

mobile.rationale: Matches Figma design where mobile logo is ~25.6px tall
mobile.note: Mobile constraint was already correct and unchanged

logoSizingExplanation.clarification: The user's requirement 'logo spans into the two rows' means the logo CONTAINER (the <a> link) spans the rows via self-stretch, creating visual alignment. The logo IMAGE inside is constrained by max-height to prevent it from growing too large. This is the correct interpretation matching Figma.

warnings: Asset size warnings (js/editor.js 348 KiB, js/screen.js) - pre-existing, not related to changes

syncStatus.notes: All three logo instances (PHP logo link, TSX ImageDropUploader placeholder, TSX logo link) now have identical classes including the desktop max-height constraint.

designQaVerification.verification: Logo will now display at ~58px height on desktop (1024px+) instead of stretching to ~500px. The self-stretch container allows the logo to span the two-row layout visually while the max-height keeps the actual logo image compact.
designQaVerification.expectedResult: Logo appears compact (~58px tall) on the left side of the header bar at desktop widths, matching Figma proportions where the logo sits beside the right-side column containing search/SEP link and nav items.

otherDesignIssues.DQA-002: Submenu gap - within tolerance, no fix needed
otherDesignIssues.DQA-003: Submenu text alignment - correct, no fix needed
otherDesignIssues.DQA-004: Focus outlines - correct, no fix needed
otherDesignIssues.DQA-005: Search placeholder - correct, no fix needed
otherDesignIssues.DQA-006: Fixed header width - correct, no fix needed
otherDesignIssues.DQA-007: Mobile layout - correct, no fix needed
otherDesignIssues.DQA-008: Desktop nav gap - correct, no fix needed

testPage.note: Test page unchanged. Logo max-height fix is a CSS-only change that will take effect immediately on page load.

lessonsLearned.misunderstanding: Initially misunderstood 'logo spans into the two rows' to mean the logo should FILL the two-row height, leading to removal of max-h constraint.
lessonsLearned.correct_interpretation: Logo CONTAINER spans rows via self-stretch for alignment, but logo IMAGE is constrained by max-height to maintain proportions per Figma.
lessonsLearned.importance_of_figma_values: Always check Figma for exact pixel values. The ~58px logo height in Figma was the key indicator that max-height was needed.
